title: US-Israel strikes on Iran: How will India be hit by Strait of Hormuz closure? Explained
content: The Strait of Hormuz has Iran on the northern side and the UAE and Oman on the southern side. It extends for around 100 miles, and its narrowest stretch is 21 miles wide. It has shallow depth, which means that ships passing through it are vulnerable to naval mines.

title: ‘In the name of Haidar’: Last Message posted on Khamenei’s X after Trump declares him dead
content: Following Donald Trump's announcement of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death in a US-Israel strike, Iran's Supreme Leader's social media posted a cryptic message invoking Haidar. Iran has not confirmed the death but vowed retaliation, launching missiles and drones at Israel and US bases. Khamenei, ruling since 1989, leaves no confirmed successor, raising concerns about political stability.

title: Batting order not fixed, says India coach ahead of must-win West Indies clash
content: Tilak had a modest run in the first five matches of the tournament while batting at one down, managing a strike-rate of 120. The team management then pushed him to No. 6 against Zimbabwe.

title: New SIM-binding rule from March 1: WhatsApp, Telegram users must know these changes
content: Starting March 1, messaging apps like WhatsApp and Telegram must implement SIM-binding, requiring services to function only with the registered SIM in the primary device. This government mandate aims to curb online fraud and enhance traceability by linking active accounts to verified SIMs. Web versions will also face stricter auto-logout rules.

title: Watch: Drone debris hits Dubai’s Burj Al Arab after Iranian strike
content: Dubai's iconic Burj Al Arab faced a minor fire after debris from an intercepted drone struck its facade. Authorities confirmed the incident, with Civil Defence swiftly controlling the blaze. This follows reports of an Iranian drone strike and damage at Dubai International Airport, prompting residents to seek shelter due to potential missile threats.

title: Khamenei killed in US strike: Who are his potential successors?
content: US President Donald Trump announced Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei's death following joint US-Israeli strikes, calling it a chance for Iranians to reclaim their nation.  Israeli officials confirmed the death, with Iranian state media reporting explosions and disruptions in Tehran.  The succession process, tightly controlled by the regime, now faces scrutiny amid regional tensions.

title: Question that Bill Gates' daughter Phoebe Gates asks every candidate she interviews
content: Phoebe Gates, co-CEO of AI shopping assistant Phia, reveals her unique interview question: 'How much do you think California state spends on healthcare? And do a bottoms-up approach for how you would build that out.' This assesses problem-solving, not just facts. She also seeks dedication by asking about the 'craziest' sales deal closure.

title: All you need to know about ‘Cobra’ actor Vikram's home in Chennai
content: Chiyaan Vikram, the acclaimed Tamil actor, embraces a simple, eco-conscious life at his Chennai home in Mahalingapuram. The residence features a minimalist design and natural materials—teakwood and marble—framed by garden pockets and inner courtyards that cultivate serenity and sustainability. His deep commitment to his craft complements a grounded personal life.

title: Bengaluru school owner held for 'raping' Class 8 student; principal among 3 arrested
content: A couple running a Devanahalli residential school and its principal were arrested under the Pocso Act following a class 8 student's sexual assault allegations. The 14-year-old survivor accused the owner of rape and claimed his wife and the principal failed to intervene, dismissing her concerns as common. Police are investigating further incidents.

title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy has a five-word reply for Jack Dorsey firing half of its staff
content: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy says he hasn't "really digested" Jack Dorsey's decision to lay off 4,000 employees — nearly half of Block's workforce — citing AI-driven efficiency gains. Jassy acknowledges that many jobs humans have done for decades won't need as many people going forward, but struck an optimistic note, saying new roles will emerge as they have with every past technology shift. He called it a "transition" period.

title: Fortnite Love and Legends Power Hour event: Release timings for all regions
content: Epic Games announced the final two Fortnite Love and Legends Power Hour events for February 28 and March 1, 2026. These events offer the most generous loot distribution, featuring four Legendary POIs per match with Rare Chests. Players have a final chance to earn Candy Hearts and themed cosmetic items during these limited-time, one-hour events.

title: Matthew Tkachuk calls Edmonton Oilers captain Connor McDavid best player in the world
content: Florida Panthers forward Matthew Tkachuk called Edmonton Oilers captain Connor McDavid the best player in the world after their Olympic gold medal showdown at the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Games. Tkachuk reflected on a pivotal breakaway save by United States goaltender Connor Hellebuyck that preserved Team USA’s lead and shifted momentum in the championship game.

title: Toronto Maple Leafs face trade deadline pressure amid Auston Matthews scrutiny
content: As the NHL trade deadline approaches, the Toronto Maple Leafs are unsure about the direction of their roster and their captain, Auston Matthews; as rumors about NHL trades grow stronger, management needs to find a balance between being competitive in the short term and planning for the long term. This week is going to be very important for the NHL. Along with a lot of big news coming out. There are questions about player availability, locker room dynamics, and Matthews' future.

title: Operation Epic Fury: How Trump-led US strikes in Tehran killed Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei
content: Operation Epic Fury, a US military action under Donald Trump, achieved its main goal. Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in strikes on Tehran. The operation also targeted Khamenei's daughter and grandchild. The offensive marked a major escalation, aiming to dismantle the Khamenei-led establishment. Explosions were reported across several Iranian cities.

title: Exiled crown prince hails ‘turning point’ after Ayatollah’s death - top developments
content: A massive cloud of smoke rose over Tehran following a daylight "preventive attack" by the United States and Israel, targeting military sites near Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's offices. Loud blasts were heard as the US confirmed its participation in the strikes, hours after President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with nuclear talks and hinted at a "big decision"

title: Iran-Israel: Drone hits residential building in Bahrain- video
content: Bahrain's capital Manama faced attacks on residential buildings. An Iranian drone struck a tower in the city. This follows Iran's missile strikes on United States military bases in Bahrain and other Gulf nations. The strikes were in retaliation for joint US-Israel airstrikes on Tehran. Civil defense teams are responding to the incidents.

title: Pakistan beat Sri Lanka but crash out of T20 World Cup; NZ enter semis on NRR
content: Pakistan's T20 World Cup journey ended despite a thrilling five-run victory over Sri Lanka. Needing to restrict Sri Lanka to 147 or less, Pakistan's bowlers conceded 207, falling short on net run rate. New Zealand secured the semifinal spot. Sahibzada Farhan's century was a highlight for Pakistan.

title: Air India cancels 28 Europe, US, Canada flights on March 1 amid Middle East tensions
content: Air India has cancelled 28 international flights to Europe, the US, and Canada on March 1st due to the escalating Middle East situation.  The airline cited safety concerns for passengers and staff following coordinated strikes in the region. Affected travelers are being notified and offered alternative arrangements to minimize disruption.

title: US–Israel strikes Iran: Indian embassies in UAE, Syria issue safety advisories for nationals tensions escalate
content: Indian embassies in the UAE and Syria have issued urgent advisories for nationals to exercise caution due to escalating Middle East tensions following US and Israeli strikes on Iran.  Stranded passengers are being assisted, while citizens in Syria are urged to avoid non-essential travel and remain vigilant amid reported counterattacks and significant casualties.

title: US, Israel attack Iran: Videos show explosion at Dubai's iconic Palm Jumeirah
content: An explosion and smoke plume were reported at Dubai's Palm Jumeirah on Saturday, injuring four people. This incident occurred amidst heightened regional tensions following Iran's retaliatory strikes across the Gulf. Dubai authorities confirmed a fire at a building, which has since been controlled. The event caused concern in the city known for luxury and tourism.

title: ‘No ship allowed’: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz; US issues advisory after strike
content: Vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz are reportedly receiving radio warnings, attributed to Iran's Revolutionary Guards, stating passage is prohibited. This critical oil route, vital for global energy supplies, faces heightened tensions following recent regional strikes. Iran has not officially confirmed issuing such an order, raising concerns for commercial shipping.

title: Satellite image shows impact of US-Israel strike on Iran Supreme Leader Khamenei's palace
content: Several key structures within the compound, long used as Khamenei's primary official residence and a central hub for leadership functions, appear obliterated or severely compromised. Iran struck American military bases across the Middle East in retaliatory strikes against Israel and the United States, hours after the two arch-rivals rocked Tehran Saturday morning.

title: Government directs cricketing body to drop 'India' or 'Indian' from name; here's why
content: Sports Ministry has directed the Differently-Abled Cricket Council of India (DCCI) to cease using "India" or "Indian" in its name and activities, as it lacks National Sports Federation (NSF) recognition. Similar notices were issued to other sports bodies. DCCI seeks NSF status, highlighting its international events and BCCI backing.

title: Bored of Peace? Half of Donald Trump's Board of Peace is at war
content: Donald Trump’s grandly titled Board of Peace was supposed to be a Trumpian alternative to the United Nations, promising order, stability, and the end of endless wars. Instead, it resembles a Yes Minister casting call where the least qualified are handed the grandest titles. Nearly half its members are now entangled in active military escalation, from US-Israel strikes on Iran to Pakistan’s growing confrontation with the Taliban. The result is an institution dedicated to peace whose members are too busy exchanging missiles to practise it. Like many international bodies before it, the Board of Peace demonstrates that peace, as ever, remains largely aspirational branding.

title: Why was Oman spared as Iran targeted Gulf states after US–Israel strikes
content: Missile strikes rocked the Gulf following US-Israeli actions against Iran, hitting Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, the UAE, and Saudi Arabia.  Oman, however, remained untouched. This neutrality, coupled with its crucial role as a diplomatic intermediary between Tehran and Washington, likely protected it from retaliation, preserving a vital, albeit fragile, communication line amidst escalating tensions.

title: Family full of pride make room to watch son Auqib make Ranji history
content: In Sheeri village near the Line of Control, residents gathered anxiously to follow the Ranji Trophy final as local boy Auqib Nabi Dar played. Inside his modest home, family members watched silently until Jammu & Kashmir won and Auqib was named Player of the Tournament. His father, once keen on a medical career for him, now proudly dreams of seeing him in the Indian jersey.

title: How flights moved out of Iranian airspace after US-Israel strikes — video
content: Commercial flights rerouted as US and Israel conducted coordinated strikes across Iran, including Tehran, following multiple explosions. Defence Minister Israel Katz termed it a 'pre-emptive' move. President Trump confirmed US involvement, aiming to curb Iran's nuclear program. Iran subsequently launched retaliatory strikes targeting Israel and US facilities, impacting several regional nations.

title: Pakistan's Farhan and Fakhar Zaman run riot; break these batting records at Pallekele
content: Fakhar Zaman and Sahibzada Farhan etched their names in T20 World Cup history with a record 176-run opening stand against Sri Lanka. This monumental partnership propelled Pakistan to a commanding 212, setting a challenging target.  Pakistan's bowlers now face the crucial task of restricting Sri Lanka to secure a semi-final berth.

title: 50+ Happy Holi wishes, messages and greetings, WhatsApp status for Holi 2026
content: India's vibrant Holi festival, celebrated in spring, embodies love and camaraderie.  Marked by smearing colors and splashing water, it's a time for families and friends to unite, sharing wishes for happiness and prosperity.  The spirit of "Bura Na Mano Holi Hai" encourages lighthearted fun, making it a joyous occasion for all.

title: Iran targets Qatar: Falling missile debris creates panic in Doha – Watch
content: Panic erupted on the outskirts of Qatar's capital, Doha, as rocket debris fell, causing a large explosion. This incident occurred amidst retaliatory strikes by Iran targeting Israel and US facilities, following earlier attacks on Iran. The event highlights the growing risk of regional conflict spillover, with Qatar hosting a key US military base and having condemned the attack.

title: Farhan surpasses Virat Kohli, becomes highest ever to score this record
content: Sahibzada Farhan broke Virat Kohli’s record for most runs in a single T20 World Cup edition during Pakistan’s must-win Super 8 clash against Sri Lanka. Kohli had scored 319 runs in 2014. Farhan’s fluent knock powered Pakistan to 150 without loss alongside Fakhar Zaman, keeping their semifinal hopes alive with a strong start.

title: US-Israel strike Iran: Why Strait of Hormuz matters for oil prices - explained
content: Escalating US-Iran tensions, including strikes and retaliation, have reignited concerns over the vital Strait of Hormuz. This narrow passage, crucial for global oil trade, faces potential disruption, threatening to send crude prices soaring. India, heavily reliant on Middle Eastern oil, could face significant economic impact if the strait is closed.

title: MSU's Thompson blazes combine-best 4.26 in 40
content: Mississippi State's Brenen Thompson clocked a 4.26-second 40-yard dash Saturday, the third fastest by a wide receiver at the combine since 2003.

title: Green, top-10 QB prospect, sets combine records
content: Taylen Green, a top-10 quarterback prospect ahead of April's NFL draft, set several positional combine records during Saturday's workout in Indianapolis.

title: Buffs asst. Sapp resigns; Marve promoted to DC
content: Colorado defensive assistant Warren Sapp resigned to "pursue other opportunities," and Chris Marve was promoted to defensive coordinator, the school announced.

title: Kerr walks back comments on Porzingis' diagnosis
content: Warriors center Kristaps Porzingis will miss a fourth straight game on Saturday night due to general illness.

title: Spoelstra: KD in '28 Games would be 'incredible'
content: Erik Spoelstra says it's incredible to think of Kevin Durant on the 2028 Olympic team if Durant still wants to play.

title: Barça's Yamal after 1st hat trick: I'm happy again
content: Lamine Yamal has revealed he has not felt good this season but says he is finally enjoying football again after scoring the first hat trick of his career

title: Michigan loses guard Cason (ACL) for rest of year
content: Michigan's L.J. Cason, who had emerged as one of the premier backup guards in college basketball this season, suffered a torn right ACL and will miss the rest of the season.

title: FIFA to 'monitor developments' in Iran ahead of WC
content: FIFA will "monitor developments" in Iran following the outbreak of military action by the United States -- the co-hosts of this summer's men's World Cup -- against the Middle Eastern nation.

title: 🏈 Best NFL combine workouts and draft risers
content: Top prospects such as David Bailey and Ohio State's linebacker duo starred. Here are our experts on the combine's top workouts.

title: Where would Fernando Mendoza rank among QBs drafted in Round 1 over the past five years?
content: Let's rank every quarterback picked in Round 1 from 2021 to 2025 based on predraft evaluations -- and then slot in the expected No. 1 pick for 2026.

title: Did Sydney Sweeney hit Bruno Fernandes' celebration on visit to Man United star's old club?
content: Hollywood star Sydney Sweeney visited Sporting CP and appeared to imitate the trademark celebration of one of the club's former player, Bruno Fernandes.

title: Late to set-piece party, Liverpool are making up for lost time
content: Liverpool were unlucky from set pieces in the first half of the season, but that turned around on Saturday.

title: Six teams, five games, one title on the line: Your guide to the Unrivaled playoffs
content: The Phantom, Laces, Mist, Breeze, Rose and Vinyl have advanced to the Unrivaled playoffs, which open Saturday.

title: Bracketology: UNC inching closer but still outside of top 16
content: The Tar Heels are in third place in the ACC and have won 11 of their past 12 games, but they remain a No. 5 seed.

title: 'You're looking at a Ferrari': Introducing Konnor Griffin, MLB's next teenage superstar
content: The No. 1 prospect in baseball has a chance to join exclusive company on Opening Day -- if the Pirates are willing to let him.

title: Ten years into his UFC career, Brandon Moreno still thinks he can be a three-time champion
content: One more run at the flyweight title would cap an already historic career for Mexican MMA pioneer Brandon Moreno.

title: One free agent each NFL team should sign: We found 32 perfect fits
content: Mike Evans to the Bills? Malik Willis to the Jets? Riq Woolen to the Jaguars? We found free agent fits for all 32 teams this offseason.

title: Betting buzz: Kon Knueppel overtakes Cooper Flagg as NBA Rookie of the Year favorite
content: Kon Knueppel's hot play has shaken up the Rookie of the Year odds, as the Hornets' star passes Cooper Flagg as the favorite.

title: 'Attempting to mingle': Cameron Brink reveals how she met her fiancé via email
content: Brinks now-fiancé got in contact via unconventional means, eschewing social media direct messages and instead sending an email.

title: Champions League round of 16 predictions: Real Madrid over Man City?
content: The path to the Champions League final is now mapped out, but who do we think makes it all the way to Budapest? Mark Ogden makes his picks.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Inter could reignite interest in Liverpool's Jones
content: Manchester City and Manchester United are set for a battle to sign Nottingham Forest's Elliot Anderson this summer. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Opposition raises SIR concerns in Gujarat Assembly debate; Speaker allows limited discussion on exercise
content: While Speaker Shankar Chaudhary had already allowed two Congress MLAs and one AAP legislator to speak on the Bill, Gujarat Forest and Environment Minister Arjun Modhwadia raised a point of order contending that matters pertaining to Election Commission of India (EC) cannot be discussed in the State Assembly

title: India braces for hotter-than-normal summer, more heatwave days expected
content: In March, ‌the first month of the summer season, minimum temperatures are ‌likely to remain above ‌average ⁠across most areas, said Mrutyunjay ⁠Mohapatra, director-general of the India Meteorological Department (IMD)

title: PV Sindhu stranded in Dubai as flights remain suspended after tensions across West Asia
content: PV Sindhu said she was safe but stuck with her team; she added that the speed at which the tensions have escalated is “terrifying”

title: Karnataka government skirts internal reservation in recruitments
content: The Government Order has asked authorities/ department to initiate recruitment notifications over the next 30 days to fill 56,432 posts with no mention of internal reservation

title: Diverse culture of States, countries portrayed at VIT’s Riviera 2026
content: At Akiya, which was started in 2015, the art and culture of 24 States was displayed with over 800 students taking part in it

title: When art wilts under the sun
content: The impacts of climate change are not for the future, they are here, now, and will only inconvenience humans more over time. Geetha Srimathi looks at how the trade and health of folk artistes of Tamil Nadu have already been deeply impacted by the growing summer heat

title: Tiruvannamalai RTO gets Automated Driving Test Track for two-wheelers, cars
content: The new testing track is equipped with advanced sensors and CCTV cameras to ensure transparent and error-free evaluation

title: For a house built in Kerala, another brought down in Bihar
content: A caste conflict in Bihar was triggered by a wage dispute over a work that was done over 2,500 kilometres away in Kerala. Amit Bhelari reports on the allegations made by both sides, which resulted in two FIRs being filed, one invoking the SC/ST Act. The brahmins had allegedly forcibly entered the home of the Scheduled Caste man who asked for money due to him. In the background, play out themes of movement and migration

title: ‘Constitutional morality finally comes down to justice without fear or favour’, Kapil Sibal at Justice Unplugged 2026
content: There is complete breakdown of constitutional machinery, says senior advocate Kapil Sibal in conversation with N. Ram, Director of The Hindu Group, at The Hindu Justice Unplugged 2026; justice needs to rise above fractious debates to interpret Constitution for the greater good, he says

title: ‘Constitutional thinking has to start from home’, says S. Muralidhar at Justice Unplugged 2026
content: Indians do not allow constitutional provisions to influence personal behaviour, says former Orissa HC Chief Justice at The Hindu Justice Unplugged 2026; change comes from social milieu, he adds

title: Why is the NEP undergrad course in chaos? | Explained
content: What are the challenges? Why are both students and teachers struggling with the fourth year schedule? Is there extra funding from UGC for the fourth year programme? What about infrastructure? Why are there frequent changes in guidelines? What lies ahead?

title: Where does the India-U.S. trade deal stand? | Explained
content: After the U.S. Supreme Court struck down the tariffs imposed by Donald Trump on countries across the world, how did the American President react? Will reciprocal tariffs be removed? Why does a 50% tariff on aluminium and steel imports continue?

title: Assam villagers resist excavation for flood mitigation project
content: Police serve notice to Raijor Dal leader Debanga Sourav Gogoi, an IIM alumnus, for allegedly instigating farmers around a network of wetlands in Jorhat district

title: ‘Justice Unplugged 2026’: extending learning beyond the classroom
content: Here’s a collection of storis from what experts and practitioners in the legal field discussed at ‘Justice Unplugged 2026’

title: Dilution of RTI Act undermining Constitutional guarantees, say legal experts
content: With greater access to court proceedings and the live-streaming of hearings, both the Bench and the Bar must exercise restraint in avoiding sensational remarks that could compromise the integrity of proceedings, experts say at ‘Justice Unplugged: Shaping the Future of Law’ conclave

title: Drive launched to make Bidar addiction-free district
content: Samiti stresses the need for collective action to eliminate social evils and to protect youth from addiction

title: Accessibility in courts remains a challenge, says lawyer
content: After Sarah Sunny’s case, things have not progressed and the process of getting an interpreter in courts is very tedious, says Sanchita Ain

title: Kalaburagi to host landmark ITF W35 women’s tennis tournament
content: The tournament will feature a 32-player singles main draw, preceded by a 32-player qualifying draw, along with a 16-team doubles draw

title: ‘First duty is to fight the system’: Advocates, judges tell law students to put Constitution first
content: Kapil Sibal speaks about courage and commitment to the profession, Abhishek Manu Singhvi comments on the NCERT textbook row, and Justice S. Muralidhar vouches for sustained self-education

title: Congress and DMK talks ‘smooth’, likely to reach an understanding soon
content: TNCC president says Congress-DMK alliance is almost sealed and they will agree upon a reasonable number of seats; he rejects speculations of a split, adds that the DMK has agreed to allot an RS seat

title: AI can be an efficient tool in the legal profession but needs rigorous monitoring, say legal experts
content: Though AI can be an efficient intern, due diligence is key to avoid fictitious case citations, says senior advocate; helpful tool to summarise voluminous records, streamline case management, reduce pendency in courts

title: RTI: ₹50,000 penalty imposed on four gram panchayat officials for delay in providing information
content: State Information Commissioner B. Venkata Singh has passed the order after hearing second appeals filed by an applicant who had not received the requested information in time

title: ‘Making justice accessible requires a systemic approach beyond formal equality‘
content: Justice is not just about dispute resolution. It is about creating a structure where every person can participate in all aspects of life with dignity, says Supreme Court lawyer Sanchita Ain

title: Slew of festivals to celebrate tribal arts, music, culture and commerce in March
content: Bharat Tribes Fest at Sundar Nursery from March 18 to 30 to showcase tribal products, including an international pavilion; Living Roots Music Festival to feature performances at Kartavya Path from March 13 to 15

title: Chhattisgarh police recovered arms, ₹46 lakh cash in a Maoist dump
content: Among the weapons purportedly recovered were a muzzle loading gun, BGL shells, INSAS rounds and non-electronic detonators, officials said.

title: Lawyer’s vigilance is society’s early warning against democratic erosion, says A.M. Singhvi
content: Mr. Singhvi drew attention to what he described as a global phenomenon of constitutional backsliding, marked by deepening polarisation and the erosion of institutional norms during law conclave

title: Opposition demands Centre to condemn attacks on Iran, protect lives of Indians in West Asia
content: Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh said for weeks, U.S. President Donald Trump maintained the charade of diplomacy and negotiations with Iran

title: Helpline to be set up for people of Karnataka stuck in West Asian countries: CM
content: The Chief Minister says he had already instructed the Resident Commissioner in Delhi to make all necessary arrangements for their safety

title: Gehlot seeks restoration of Congress regime’s welfare schemes in Rajasthan
content: In letter to PM ahead of his Ajmer visit, former CM reminds Mr. Modi of his poll promise that the BJP would not shut down any Congress scheme if it came to power

title: Former ISRO chief urges students to harness emerging technologies to become future leaders
content: By embracing science and innovation, students can help the nation achieve the vision of Viksit Bharat by 2047, says A.S. Kiran Kumar

title: Canada-India negotiating economic partnership agreement to double two-way trade by 2030: PM Carney
content: “This is an enormous opportunity for both our countries.. but it is one that is about to move to the next level. We should aim much higher, and we are aiming much higher, and to be more strategic in our partnership,” Mr. Carney said

title: Nepali parties promise big ahead of polls; however, concerns remain
content: Parties have already unveiled their manifestos and are now on campaign trails, pledging not only development but also an end to corruption and good governance

title: Kapil Sibal calls for purposive interpretation of Constitution at law conclave
content: Says Constitutional morality ultimately is justice without fear or favour, true justice lies in the ability to rise above factional debates and interpret the Constitution in a manner that serves the larger community

title: PM Modi inaugurates Micron ATMP plant in Gujarat’s Sanand
content: The facility is the highest rupee beneficiary of funds from the India Semiconductor Mission, with a 50% capital subsidy from the Union government, and a further 20% from the Gujarat government

title: Action to be taken against building lacking mandatory parking space
content: The Hubballi Dharwad Municipal Corporation will soon start taking action to restore the mandatory parking space in commercial buildings and complexes and a Hubballi Dharwad Mayor Jyoti Patil issued ruling in this regard during the monthly council meeting in Dharwad on Friday

title: Kejriwal to address public rally at Jantar Mantar
content: The former Delhi Chief Minister will ‘present his views and outline the direction in which he will steer the party’, the AAP’s Delhi president Saurabh Bharadwaj says

title: Bihar police roll out comprehensive security plan ahead of Holi
content: Additional forces deployed across sensitive areas, religious sites and Naxal-affected districts; strict action warned against hooliganism, biker gangs and communal troublemakers

title: SLBC tunnel work resumes from outlet (Devarakonda) side
content: Minister for Irrigation Uttam Kumar Reddy says 20 metres excavated in a week in the rapid progress method

title: Govt. blocks Supabase website, popular among code developers
content: The site is used as a backend by several developers working on independent projects; a govt. official declined to discuss the specific reasons for the website’s blocking, but said that ‘information was being shared that should not have been shared’

title: Peace meeting held in Bagalkot on Saturday after protest on Friday night
content: In Bagalkot, where residents staged protest on Friday night over the arrest of Hindutva activists, a conciliation peace meeting was held on Saturday in which P.C. Gaddigoudar, MP, former MLA Viranna Charantimath, senior police officials and residents of Killa Galli took part and it was resolved to maintain peace

title: ‘No Pongala should be offered on granite near Padmanabhaswamy temple entrances’
content: The superior quality granite that had been laid in the traditional style need to be preserved carefully, says a temple statement

title: II PU student dies of heart attack
content: In a tragic incident, a girl student who was about to appear for her II year PUC examination died of heart attack as she was getting ready to go to the examination hall in Belagavi district on Saturday.

title: Leaders in Jammu and Kashmir condemn U.S., Israel attack on Iran
content: Scores of protesters took to the streets in Kashmir’s Bandipora to show support for Iran and condemn the attack on it by raising pro-Iran slogans; similar scenes were witnessed in Budgam and Srinagar districts

title: Double life sentence for priest convicted in POCSO case
content: Priest accused of sexually assaulting a minor under the pretext of performing rituals to ‘drive away evil spirits’

title: In a first, fuel surcharge to be refunded to KSEB consumers
content: Monthly-billed consumers will be refunded 2 paise per unit and bi-monthly consumers, 1 paise per unit, through their bills for March

title: Congress mulls fielding AICC secretary Mathew Antony from Kanjirappally
content: The move comes amid speculations that the BJP plans to field Union Minister George Kurian from the Assembly seat; Mr. Antony is the AICC secretary in charge of Meghalaya, Mizoram, and Arunachal Pradesh

title: ISRO chairman lauds space scientist B.N. Suresh’s contribution to Indian space programme
content: ISRO chairman V. Narayanan released the Kannada translation of B.N. Suresh’s biography in Shivamogga on Saturday

title: KSWMP launches ‘Clean Kerala Anthem’
content: The anthem is designed to engage all age groups, especially the youth, with the message “My Waste, My Responsibility; My Kerala, My Pride”. It encourages a cultural shift towards responsible waste management and segregation.

title: Telangana urges KRMB to stop A.P. from taking up new projects, expansions
content: ENC terms river board’s letter to TG “wholly untenable and misplaced”, while turning blind on AP’s new projects, expansions

title: NWKRTC to be named after Rani Chennamma soon, says Minister Ramalinga Reddy
content: The Minister has issued appointment orders to the newly recruited employees and awarded silver medals to drivers for accident-free driving

title: CPI(M) district secretariat suggests M.M. Mani’s candidature in Udumbanchola
content: It was earlier rumoured that the veteran leader may not contest due to health concerns. The party faced major setbacks in the local body election in Idukki, prompting a rethink

title: Kochi Corporation mulls short-term direct operation of Ro-Ro services before engaging new operator
content: Citing mounting losses, the civic body is weighing interim control before entrusting the service to a new agency. The Budget for 2026–27 has proposed forming a special purpose vehicle to manage operations, drawing criticism from the LDF over alleged privatisation

title: ‘Investment in mass rapid transit essential in making a city more liveable’
content: Interview: Andrew Barr, Minister for Tourism and Trade, Australia, and Chief Minister, Australian Capital Territory

title: Is Middle East entering a new phase of multipolar confrontation?
content: the united states and Israel launched coordinated airstrikes on Iran on saturday . they targeted military sites across the country in what both governments described as a pre-emptive operation . retaliatory missile strikes were reported across Tehran, with the Israeli military stressed it was targeting military sites .

title: 20 dead, most of them women, in Andhra Pradesh's worst firecracker unit explosion
content: a massive explosion occurred at a firecracker manufacturing unit at Vetlapalem village in Andhra Pradesh's Kakinada district on Saturday afternoon . the toll could rise as many of the injured were unlikely to survive . most of the workers in the unit were women from nearby villages .

title: India could soon get its first openly queer MP
content:
Himanshi Dhawan is a Senior Editor with The Times of India with 25 years of experience in reporting on politics, social sector, gender and marginalised communities. Her writing spans issues related to human rights violations, and looking at health, education, and livelihoods from a gendered lens. In recognition for her reporting she has received the UNFPA-Population First Laadli award for gender sensitive reporting in 2010-11. She has also been the recipient of the Edward Murrow Fellowship (2007) by the US State Department’s International Visitor Leadership Program. 
She has studied English Literature at St Stephen's College, DU and completed her post-graduation in mass communication from Sophia College, Mumbai. Read More

title: US-Israel Attacks Iran LIVE Updates: "Greatest Chance For Iranians To Take Back Their Country": Trump After Khamenei's Death
content: US-Israel Attacks Iran LIVE Updates: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in an attack on Iran launched by Israel and US, President Donald Trump said, calling it the "greatest chance" for Iranian people to "take back" their nation

title: Ayatollah Khamenei Killed In Strikes, Iran's State TV Confirms Trump's Claim
content: Ayatollah Khamenei Is Dead: After Trump, Iran's State TV Confirms Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei Killed In Strikes

content: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei's Daughter, Son-In-Law, Iran's Defence Minister Among Those Killed In Israel-US Strikes

title: Video: Dubai International Airport Damaged In Iranian Strikes, 4 Injured
content: The world's busiest international airport in Dubai was hit on Saturday following a wave of Iranian missile attacks in response to US-Israel strikes on Tehran and other Iranian cities.

title: Ayatollah Khamenei's Cryptic X Post After Trump Claims Iran's Supreme Leader Is Dead
content: Soon after American President Donald Trump and Israeli officials claimed that Ayatollah Ali Khamenei had been killed in US-Israeli strikes in Tehran, the Iranian Supreme Leader's X account posted a cryptic message.

title: Exclusive: How War In A Narrow Sea Could Reshape Global Economy Overnight
content: Geographically, the Strait of Hormuz is a narrow maritime corridor connecting the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea, and ultimately the Indian Ocean. Iran lies to the north, while Oman and the United Arab Emirates lie to the south.

title: Strait Of Hormuz: Iran's 50-Km-Wide Geopolitical Bargaining Chip
content: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Office has said it received reports from merchant ships operating in the Arabian Gulf that the Strait of Hormuz has been closed. These reports could not be independently verified at this time, it said.

title: First Visual Of US-Israel Strike On Iran Supreme Leader Khamenei's Compound
content: Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was the target of Saturday'sjoint Israeli-US strikes on Tehran, Israel's public broadcaster reported.

title: "Khamenei, One Of The Most Evil People In History, Is Dead": Donald Trump
content: Hours after the US-Israel's joint military operation, US President Donald Trump claimed on Saturday that Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah was dead.

title: Explained: How Will US, Israeli Strikes On Iran Affect Oil Markets?
content: The US-Israeli strikes against Iran and reprisals by Tehran could severely disrupt the global supply of crude oil and send prices soaring to levels not seen in years.

title: As Trump Declares Khamenei Dead, When Was Iran's Supreme Leader Last Seen?
content: In what could very well be a turning point in the history of Iran and the Middle East, US President Donald Trump claimed that Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was dead following the joint US-Israeli military operation against Tehran.

title: Burgers, Then War: Donald Trump's Bluffs Before Iran Strikes
content: On a visit to Texas on Friday, US President Donald Trump gave a raucous speech on the economy and then stopped off at a fast food joint.

title: Madhya Pradesh Police Trainees' Reel Sparks Storm, Show-Cause Notices Issued
content: The obsession with creating reels inside the police force refuses to fade and has landed six newly recruited constables in serious trouble.

title: Dubai Says Drone Debris Causes Fire At Burj Al Arab Hotel
content: Dubai authorities said debris from an intercepted drone caused a fire at the city's famed luxury hotel the Burj Al Arab, according to a statement Sunday, following a wave of Iranian strikes launched at Gulf states.

title: Madhya Pradesh Slashes Temple Renovation Budget by Two-Thirds In 3 Years
content: At a time when Madhya Pradesh speaks proudly of its two Jyotirlingas, four Shakti Peeths, and thousands of ancient temples radiating spiritual energy, a stark financial picture has emerged from the Assembly floor.

title: Dubai Airport Confirms 4 Injured In "Incident" As Iran Continues Strikes
content: Dubai airport, the world's busiest for international traffic, was damaged and four staff were injured in an "incident", it said on Saturday following waves of Iranian missile attacks.

title: All About Iran's Khamenei: Ruthless Revolutionary Atop Islamic Republic
content: Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei, a pillar of its theocratic system since the Islamic revolution whose death was announced by US President Donald Trump on Saturday, saw off multiple crises over the decades, remaining defiant to the very end.

title: Lift Plunges From Bhopal Building Days After Body Found In Shaft, 2 Injured
content: Days after a 77-year-old man's decomposed body was found inside an elevator shaft, another lift accident has been reported at Chinar Dream Cityin Bhopal.

title: How the top QB signings will fit in 2026
content: the top 2026 recruits at each position could fit at their new schools . the perfect harmony between skill and scheme can lead to production and wins on the field . this is the most high-profile prospect Houston has signed in the modern era .

title: The game that will define the 2026 season for every power-conference team
content: the season-defining game will be for all 68 power-conference teams in 2026 . peruse BC's schedule given last year's struggles, and it's tough to find many wins . the Eagles open on the road at Cincinnati, then play FCS Maine .

title: Does anyone beat Ohtani? Buster Olney ranks the top 10 designated hitters
content: coach Buster Olney ranked the best of the best at designated hitters . the goal of this exercise is to identify the best players for the 2026 season, not who might be best in five years or over their career . we rolled out a position per day over two weeks .

title: NFL Players Association releases 2026 team report cards
content: ESPN obtained the results of the NFL Players Association's 2026 report cards . it grades franchises from A-plus to F-minus on everything from ownership to treatment of families . all players who were on a 2025 roster at the time of the survey were eligible to participate .

title: Power Rankings: Stakes are high for UCLA, two teams rejoin the list
content: the Bruins will be just the fourth program to finish with a perfect regular season in the Big Ten women's college basketball . the last to do it was Maryland in 2015, the season the Terps joined the big Ten and advanced to the final Four for the second year in a row .

title: Men's Bubble Watch: Kentucky, Clemson add key wins on loaded Saturday slate
content: the 2026 men's NCAA tournament is rapidly approaching . teams have roughly two weeks until selection Sunday to strengthen their cases . we'll use a variety of metrics as guides, classifying teams based on how likely they are to make the field of 68 .

title: Matt Holliday's Stillwater estate listed for $12 million
content: a Stillwater, Oklahoma, home that once belonged to an MLB champion and seven-time All-Star is on the market at $12 million . the home features sports-related amenities such as a professionally lit baseball field in the front yard, a putting green in the backyard, and a $2 million, two-level swimming pool . indoor batting cages, pickleball and basketball courts reside in a detached $150,000 guest home on the property .

title: India walks tightrope amid Iran conflict, voices concern, urges restraint
